name: Name

promptLibrary.title: Prompt library
promptLibrary.subtitle: Learn proven prompting techniques and save reusable prompts for your team.
promptLibrary.deleteMessage: This prompt will be permanently removed from your library.

practices.title: 10 Best Prompt Practices
practices.cardDescription: Learn how to write clear, structured prompts that get better answers on the first try.
practices.intro: Strong prompts are clear, structured, and repeatable. Use these principles to get better answers on the first try — then save your best prompts below.

beSpecific.title: Be specific about the outcome
beSpecific.body: State exactly what you want: format, length, audience, and tone. "Write a 150-word LinkedIn post for HR leaders about async meetings" beats "write a post about meetings."

provideContext.title: Provide relevant context
provideContext.body: Include background the model needs: your role, product, constraints, or prior decisions. Context reduces guesswork and hallucinations.

defineRole.title: Define a role or perspective
defineRole.body: Ask the model to act as a specialist — for example, "You are a senior product marketer" or "You are a patient technical tutor." Roles shape vocabulary and depth.

specifyFormat.title: Specify the output format
specifyFormat.body: Request bullets, tables, JSON, step lists, or markdown headings when structure matters. Explicit formats make outputs easier to scan and reuse.

useExamples.title: Use examples when helpful
useExamples.body: Show one or two examples of the style or structure you want. Few-shot examples are especially useful for classification, rewriting, and templates.

breakDownTasks.title: Break complex work into steps
breakDownTasks.body: For multi-part tasks, numbered steps keep the model focused: research → outline → draft → critique. You can run each step in a follow-up message.

setConstraints.title: Set constraints and guardrails
setConstraints.body: Say what to avoid: jargon, speculation, unsupported claims, or topics outside scope. Constraints improve safety and relevance.

iterateRefine.title: Iterate and refine
iterateRefine.body: Treat the first answer as a draft. Ask for shorter versions, stronger hooks, more citations, or a different audience — small follow-ups often beat one giant prompt.

useVariables.title: Use variables for reusable prompts
useVariables.body: Replace changing details with placeholders like {{topic}} or {{audience}}. Saved variable prompts let you run the same workflow with different inputs.

reviewOutput.title: Review before you ship
reviewOutput.body: Check facts, names, numbers, and tone before sharing AI output externally. Prompting improves quality; your judgment ensures it's right.

bulkChats.confirmMessage: This will permanently delete {{count}} chat(s) and their messages. This cannot be undone.

models.sonarHint: Best for most questions — fast, focused answers with key sources (recommended).
models.sonarDeepHint: Multi-step exhaustive search — slower and longer. Use for complex topics, not simple definitions.

apiKeysModal.title: API Keys
apiKeysModal.description: Connect LLM providers to power chat. OpenRouter is recommended — it routes to many models with one key. You can also add direct keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, or Perplexity.
apiKeysModal.developerDescription: Create tokens for programmatic access to KathGPT APIs.
apiKeysModal.newKeyCopyReminder: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again.
apiKeysModal.needsResave: Your saved key could not be read (likely after an app update). Paste your API key again and click Save.
apiKeysModal.priorityNote: When OpenRouter is configured, all models route through OpenRouter. Direct provider keys are only used when OpenRouter is not set.

openrouter.name: OpenRouter
openrouter.description: One key for GPT, Claude, Gemini, and more. Recommended.
